Type of Therapy: {"Type of Therapy": "Participants were randomized to either Androgen Deprivation Therapy (ADT) plus Docetaxel (D) or ADT alone. ADT consisted of orchiectomy or luteinizing hormone-releasing hormone agonists, alone or combined with nonsteroidal androgen receptor inhibitors. Docetaxel was administered at 75 mg/m² for 21 days, up to nine cycles."}

COE_RCT_IND_OVERALL_RJ: {"COE_RCT_IND_OVERALL_RJ": "The overall risk judgment for the evidence presented in this randomized controlled trial, especially concerning the non-significant survival improvements in subgroups, indicates a high risk of bias or low certainty. This is attributed to several methodological issues, including the retrospective analysis of metastatic extent, insufficient statistical power for subgroup analyses (being 'clearly underpowered'), the study not being initially designed for such post-hoc analyses, and strong departures from proportional hazards assumptions."}
PFS Rate (%): {"PFS Rate (%) | Overall | Treatment": "For biochemical progression-free survival (bPFS): Median PFS was 22.9 months (95% CI, 19.5-28.4). The hazard ratio for the comparison of ADT plus D vs ADT was 0.67 (95% CI, 0.54–0.84; p < 0.001). For radiographic progression-free survival (rPFS): Median PFS was 22.9 months (95% CI, 20.5-31.4). The hazard ratio for the comparison of ADT plus D vs ADT was 0.69 (95% CI, 0.55–0.87; p = 0.002).", "PFS Rate (%) | Overall | Control": "For biochemical progression-free survival (bPFS): Median PFS was 12.9 months (95% CI, 11.9–17.7). The hazard ratio for the comparison of ADT plus D vs ADT was 0.67 (95% CI, 0.54–0.84; p < 0.001). For radiographic progression-free survival (rPFS): Median PFS was 15.3 months (95% CI, 12.4-19.8). The hazard ratio for the comparison of ADT plus D vs ADT was 0.69 (95% CI, 0.55–0.87; p = 0.002)."}
